steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content:
Because $x$ and $y$ are positive, $\sqrt{y^{12}}=y^6$. Then simplify by subtracting exponents for like bases:
$$\frac{3x^{19}y^7}{5x^3\sqrt{y^{12}}}=\frac{3x^{19}y^7}{5x^3y^6}$$
$$=\frac{3}{5}x^{16}y$$

steps.title: Compare both statements
steps.content: Statement I has coefficient $\frac{1}{3}$ and fractional exponents, so it does not match $\frac{3}{5}x^{16}y$. Statement II has $x^{12}y^2$, which also does not match $x^{16}y$. Neither statement is equivalent. The answer is D.
